docs.title: AI Foundry Model Availability
docs.text:
Azure AI Foundry operations

Track model availability, regional coverage, deployment SKUs, and retirement risk from one searchable dashboard.

Browse models Review retirements 87 Models Tracked 32 Azure Regions 12 Action Needed 41 Scheduled Retirements Recent availability changes Latest Availability Digest

Latest run: Jun 10, 2026. Changes are grouped by model, deployment SKU, and affected regions.

docs.title: Deprecation & Retirement Notices
docs.text:
Retiring Within 30 Days — 2 model versions

These models require immediate migration. After the retirement date, API calls will return errors.

Model Version Category Retirement Date Status Replacement o1 2024-12-17 Text Generation 2026-07-15 Retiring Soon o3 o3-mini 2025-01-31 Text Generation 2026-08-02 Retiring Soon o4-mini

Upcoming Retirements (31-90 Days) — 1 model version

Plan and test your migration to the replacement model.

Model Version Category Retirement Date Status Replacement o1-pro 2025-03-19 Text Generation 2026-09-18 Retiring o3-pro

Already Retired — 9 model versions

These models are no longer available. Migrate to the listed replacement.

Model Version Category Retirement Date Status Replacement gpt-5-chat 2025-08-07 Text Generation 2026-03-01 Retired gpt-5.2-chat gpt-5-chat 2025-10-03 Text Generation 2026-03-01 Retired gpt-5.2-chat gpt-4o 2024-05-13 Text Generation 2026-03-31 Retired gpt-5.1 gpt-4o 2024-08-06 Text Generation 2026-03-31 Retired gpt-5.1 gpt-4o 2024-11-20 Text Generation 2026-06-05 Retired gpt-5.1 gpt-4o-mini 2024-07-18 Text Generation 2026-03-31 Retired gpt-4.1-mini gpt-4o-audio-preview 2024-12-17 Audio 2026-02-02 Retired gpt-audio gpt-4o-realtime-preview 2024-12-17 Audio 2026-02-02 Retired gpt-realtime dall-e-3 3 Image And Video 2026-02-18 Retired gpt-image-1-mini Lifecycle signals

Microsoft Foundry models move through predictable stages so teams can test replacements before old versions stop serving traffic. Use this dashboard for dates and model-level risk, then validate active deployments with the Models API and Azure Service Health.

1 Preview Experimental. Suitable for evaluation, not production. 2 Generally available Production-ready. Retirement date is set at launch. 3 Legacy Newer models exist. Begin replacement evaluation. 4 Deprecated Existing customers can continue. New customers are blocked. 5 Retired Removed from service. Inference returns 410 Gone. GA model retirement path
  1. Launch GA starts Retirement date is set about 18 months out and exposed by the Models API.
  2. 12 mo Deprecated Existing subscriptions can continue; new customers cannot access that version.
  3. 90 d Replacement named Replacement is typically available in Global Standard for evaluation.
  4. 30 d Provisioned window Provisioned regions get a short manual migration window.
  5. Retire Traffic stops Requests to the retired version fail. Retirement dates are not extended.
Preview model retirement path
  1. Preview Not for production Launches with a not-sooner-than retirement date, often around 90 days out.
  2. 30 d Notice period Customers receive at least 30 days notice before upgrade or retirement.
  3. Upgrade Force-upgrade or end Preview deployments move to a newer preview or GA model, or retire if no replacement exists.
Start watching A model enters Legacy or Deprecated, or appears as Scheduled in this dashboard. Start testing The replacement is declared, usually 90-120 days before retirement. Expect notifications GA retirements get at least 60 days active notice; preview models get at least 30 days. Check deployment type Global Standard, Data Zone Standard, and Standard can auto-upgrade. Provisioned deployments must be migrated manually. Read API status carefully In the Models API, Deprecating means deprecated; Deprecated means retired.

Adapted from Microsoft Foundry Models lifecycle and support policy. See full retirement details for model-specific dates.

docs.title: Models by SKU Type
docs.text:
Explore every deployment SKU and discover which models and regions support it.

20 SKU Types 87 Models 458 Model × SKU Combinations

docs.title: SKU Deployment Types Explained
docs.text:
Global — Worldwide availability with intelligent routing

Routes requests intelligently across Azure regions for maximum availability and throughput. Data may be processed in any region within the Azure geography.

Models 87 Regions 32 (100% coverage)

SKU types: Global Standard · Global batch · Global batch datazone · Global coverage

Best for: Applications needing worldwide reach, automatic failover, and maximum uptime across Azure's global network.

Compliance: Data may cross region boundaries — not suitable for HIPAA, FedRAMP, or strict data-residency requirements.

Datazone — Data residency compliance deployments

Keeps data within a specified geographic zone to satisfy compliance and residency policies. Choose the zone; Azure handles routing within that boundary.

Models 37 Regions 16 (50% coverage)

SKU types: Datazone provisioned managed · Datazone standard

Best for: GDPR compliance, data sovereignty requirements, regulated industries (finance, healthcare, government).

Compliance: Data stays within the specified geographic zone — supports GDPR and regional data-residency policies.

Standard — Pay-as-you-go regional deployments

Pay-as-you-go deployments in a single Azure region with flexible, on-demand scaling. No capacity reservation required — you pay only for what you use.

Models 13 Regions 25 (78% coverage)

SKU types: Standard

Best for: Variable workloads, development and testing, cost-sensitive applications, or when you don't need guaranteed throughput.

Compliance: Single-region deployment — HIPAA-eligible in supported regions with a BAA from Microsoft.

Provisioned (PTU) — Reserved throughput capacity

Reserved throughput units (PTUs) guarantee consistent, high-performance inference at scale. Capacity is pre-allocated, so latency and throughput are predictable regardless of platform load.

Models 22 Regions 31 (97% coverage)

SKU types: Global Provisioned Managed · Provisioned (PTU managed) · Provisioned global

Best for: High-volume production workloads, latency-sensitive applications, or scenarios where consistent throughput is critical.

Compliance: Single-region deployment — HIPAA-eligible in supported regions with a BAA from Microsoft.

SKU Selection Guide Need Recommended SKU Why Global reach with failover Global Automatic routing, high availability Data residency compliance Datazone Data stays in specified regions Cost-effective, variable load Standard Pay-as-you-go pricing Predictable high throughput Provisioned (PTU) Reserved capacity, guaranteed performance HIPAA / regulated workloads Standard or Provisioned Single-region; HIPAA-eligible with a Microsoft BAA Avoid Global for compliance ⚠ Not Global Global data routing is incompatible with strict data-residency requirements SKU Overview Category SKU Type Models Regions Coverage Global Global Standard 80 31 97% Global Global batch 10 22 69% Global Global batch datazone 10 12 38% Global Global coverage 87 32 100% Datazone Datazone provisioned managed 16 13 41% Datazone Datazone standard 36 16 50% Standard Standard 13 25 78% Provisioned Global Provisioned Managed 4 30 94% Provisioned Provisioned (PTU managed) 15 27 84% Provisioned Provisioned global 18 27 84% Other Datazone Provisioned Managed Gov 3 1 3% Other Datazone Standard Gov 5 1 3% Other Datazone Standard Priority Processing 6 7 22% Other Deployments Batch 10 22 69% Other Deployments Provisioned 19 28 88% Other Deployments Standard 60 28 88% Other Provisioned Models Gov 1 1 3% Other Standard Global By Capability 53 28 88% Other Standard Global Priority Processing 6 27 84% Other Standard Models Gov 6 1 3%

docs.text:
Plan your migrations for these models.

gpt-4o and gpt-4o-mini Retirement Update

Standard deployment type retires 2026-03-31, with auto-upgrades starting 2026-03-09. For Provisioned, Global Standard, and Data Zone Standard deployment types, the retirement date has been moved to 2026-10-01.

For more details, see the Azure AI Foundry model retirements documentation.

docs.title: Fine-Tuned Model Retirements
docs.text:
Fine-tuned models retire in two phases: training and deployment.

Model Version Training Retirement Deployment Retirement gpt-4o 2024-08-06 No earlier than 2026-09-31 (For existing customers only) 2027-03-31 gpt-4o-mini 2024-07-18 No earlier than 2026-09-31 (For existing customers only) 2027-03-31 gpt-4.1 2025-04-14 Base model retirement One year after training retirement gpt-4.1-mini 2025-04-14 Base model retirement One year after training retirement gpt-4.1-nano 2025-04-14 Base model retirement One year after training retirement o4-mini 2025-04-16 Base model retirement One year after training retirement

Fine-Tuning Retirement Policy

  • Training retirement: After this date, you cannot create new fine-tuned models.
  • Deployment retirement: After this date, inference and deployment return errors.
  • Models you've already trained remain available until deployment retirement.

docs.text: Status Description Action Required Retiring Soon Within 30 days Immediate migration required Retiring 31-90 days Plan and test migration Scheduled 91+ days Monitor and prepare Planned Date not finalized Stay informed Retired No longer available Migration required
